title: News article paragraph
description: Full news paragraph — sustained journalistic speed
text: Global markets opened higher on Wednesday after central banks signaled a softer approach to interest rates. Tech stocks led the rally, with major indexes climbing more than 1.5% by midday. Analysts noted that volatility could return if inflation data surprises on Friday.

tips.text: News prose is built to carry facts and nothing spare. Clear, tight, every word earning its place. This is the work I know best.
A figure or a percentage drops into the middle of a sentence and the line has to keep moving. Flow through it; don't stall.

tips.target: Global markets opened higher on Wednesday after central banks signaled a softer approach to interest rates. Tech stocks led the rally,

tips.text: Hold the pace from the first word to the last. A paragraph that starts fast and limps to the end is a paragraph that lost its nerve.

tips.target: with major indexes climbing more than 1.5% by midday. Analysts noted that volatility could return if inflation data surprises on Friday.

character_tips.anna: Real news writing is fast, dense, and exact. It was my whole working life. Type it well and you can type anything. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.maxim: News is the hardest plain prose there is - dense, factual, and always against the clock. Good training for the nerve. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.knopych: No need to shout breaking news at yourself. The copy is calm; let your hands be calm too. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.klavochka: You are typing the day's story now. Keep it even and let the facts speak for themselves. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
